records.summary: Form analysis, diagnostics, and repair work should reuse observer locator and binding value-channel semantics before adding feature-local form logic.
records.rationale: Form pressure appears in app-pattern fixtures, stress pressure fixtures, and external apps. The reusable substrate is observer/value-channel/data-flow modeling, not a generator-specific form model.
guidance: For compact form/control explanation, inspect `BindingValueChannelSummary` before paging raw value-channel rows; it groups channel kinds and `observerCouplings` for MCP/LSP answers that need low-token observer mechanism context, with capped representative sets plus counts.

Pair `BindingDataFlowSummary` with `BindingValueChannelSummary` when the question is source/target flow, assignment strictness, source roots, or framework error-code pressure rather than observer-channel mechanism alone; use `page.size=0` on summary queries for rollup-only first reads. Data-flow issue rows split unresolved source typing, nullish source/target write-direction mismatches, and empty-array inferred targets so MCP/LSP repair routing can pick source narrowing, optional receiving-side contracts, or explicit target typing before opening exact rows.
For form completions or diagnostics, inspect value-channel rows, binding data-flow rows, and observer locator rows together.

Binding value-channel API rows carry `targetKind`; Binding value-channel summaries use that to split native element `value` observers from custom element/component `value`/`model`/`checked` APIs instead of inferring custom controls from target names alone. Null target channels are open/unknown, not native DOM observer evidence.

When a form target-to-source diagnostic is only TypeScript strictness, guide by the observer channel: single select/radio `value` attributes write strings, `model.bind` preserves app domain values, checked collections/maps need element/key type alignment, and native value observers usually write strings.
If weakly typed app code prevents autocomplete, preserve the weakness and surface repair/diagnostic guidance instead of guessing.
Keep leaf user-control exceptions as app-building taste policy; do not turn them into universal semantic truth.

records.summary: Observation issues own framework observation errors and valid-code observation hazards that belong below app diagnostics.
records.rationale: The runtime @astTrack, @computed, and @observable decorators throw from observation code, even on ordinary classes. Binding observation can also identify valid TypeScript/Aurelia code that will not update as intended, such as template calls into undecorated methods with hidden `this.*` reads. Semantic-runtime models both as observation-owned issues rather than squeezing them into resource metadata, template diagnostics, or API-level wording.
guidance: Start here when a framework error row comes from observation-authored TypeScript such as @astTrack or @observable decorator context rather than from a template binding target.

As of 2026-05-14, non-method @astTrack targets spend runtime ast_track_decorator_not_a_method / AUR0117; invalid @observable forms spend runtime invalid_observable_decorator_usage / AUR0224; invalid @computed(...) non-getter/non-method targets spend runtime-html computed_not_getter / AUR0228.

As of 2026-06-10, non-trackable template method calls publish semantic-runtime-product warning rows with issueKind `non-trackable-template-method-call` after binding data-flow observed-dependency materialization. The warning is source-backed at the template call, includes related sources for the called Program-owned method and direct hidden body reads, and carries a `make-method-trackable` suggestion; a future code-action/edit surface must still decide the exact source edit. Do not treat this as complete transitive method-body analysis yet.
Keep import/decorator recognition exact and source-grounded; broaden the shared import-binding helper before adding local alias readers.
Publish ObservationIssue products low in the observation substrate, then let app diagnostics and repair planning consume those rows.
Do not claim framework-error authority from API wording. Check the Aurelia framework usage row and add exact links only where the source-level observation path is modeled.
